subject: Sunday call
snippet: Amanda, your mother and I will be by the phone Sunday afternoon like always...
body:
Amanda, your mother and I will be by the phone Sunday afternoon like always - don't let a client deadline swallow the whole day. She's making egusi and wants to hear how the Stonewick pentest went.

And send us that new AmandaXO track when you get a minute. Ada keeps talking about it but we want to hear it ourselves.

subject: Re: Sunday call
snippet: Thanks Dad, I'll be on the phone Sunday for sure - I already blocked the aft...
body:
Thanks Dad, I'll be on the phone Sunday for sure - I already blocked the afternoon so no client can touch it. Tell Mom I've been dreaming about her egusi.

Stonewick went well, wrapped the report Friday, no critical findings. And I'll drop the new AmandaXO record in the family chat tonight - it's called Lagos Sunset, Yemi sang the hook. I think you two will love it.

Talk Sunday!

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: Hey! Are you free for lunch any day this week? I've got a meeting in Greensb...
body:
Hey! Are you free for lunch any day this week? I've got a meeting in Greensboro Thursday so I could swing by that jollof spot on Tate Street you're always raving about. Been thinking about it nonstop.

Let me know what works. I'm flexible Thursday or Friday - figured we could catch up before your bday dinner planning gets crazy.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Yes, Tate Street it is! Thursday works perfectly - I can meet around 12:30 o...
body:
Yes, Tate Street it is! Thursday works perfectly - I can meet around 12:30 once I wrap my client call.

Heads up, I might be five minutes late because I've got a Greenleaf HIPAA readout at noon and those always run long. But I'll text you if anything changes. Bringing my gym bag too so we can hit Piedmont Iron after if you're up for it.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: 12:30 is perfect, no worries about the call running over. I'll grab us a tab...
body:
12:30 is perfect, no worries about the call running over. I'll grab us a table and order some puff-puff while I wait - I know you love those.

And yeah, I'm in for Piedmont Iron after. Just don't try to out-bench me again lol. See you Thursday, feels like forever since we actually caught up properly.

subject: Is my tutoring site safe?
snippet: Hi Amanda, I hope you're doing well! I've been putting together a simple website...
body:
Hi Amanda, I hope you're doing well! I've been putting together a simple website for my church's community tutoring program, and before I put it online I want to make sure I'm not doing anything careless with people's information.

You do security for a living, so I figured you'd be the perfect person to ask. There's a little contact form where parents type in their names and phone numbers. Is that safe? Do I need to worry about it getting hacked or leaking? I'm also not sure if the layout even makes sense on a phone.

No rush at all, but if you get a chance this weekend that would be great. Love you!

Emeka

subject: Re: Is my tutoring site safe?
snippet: Dad! Of course I'd love to help. Send over the link whenever you're ready an...
body:
Dad! Of course I'd love to help. Send over the link whenever you're ready and I'll take a look.

For the contact form, the main thing is making sure the site runs over HTTPS and that the form goes somewhere secure, not just an open spreadsheet anyone can find. If it's collecting kids' info at all we should be a little extra careful. I'll poke at it the way I do for my Greenleaf and Stonewick clients - nothing invasive, just checking the basics.

Who hosts it, and are you using any form builder? That'll tell me what to look for. Talk soon!

Amanda

subject: Re: Is my tutoring site safe?
snippet: It's on one of those simple website builders, and I used their built-in cont...
body:
It's on one of those simple website builders, and I used their built-in contact form. I'm not clever enough to have set up anything fancy myself! There are three pages - Home, About, and Contact. The Contact one is where parents type their info.

I've attached the link here: [link]. Poke at it however you need to. I'd rather you find a problem now than have some stranger find it later.

I really appreciate you doing this, sweetheart. You always look out for us.

Emeka

subject: Re: Is my tutoring site safe?
snippet: Okay, I went through all three pages and ran a few of my usual checks. Good ...
body:
Okay, I went through all three pages and ran a few of my usual checks. Good news: the site is on HTTPS and the form submissions go to the builder's own backend, not an open sheet, so you're in decent shape. Two things to fix before you go live: turn on the spam/captcha option on the Contact form so bots don't flood it, and don't collect kids' full names plus phone numbers if you can avoid it - a parent email is enough. Less data to protect is always safer.

On mobile the Home page text is a bit small, but that's minor. Once you tighten up that form I think it's ready. Happy to do a second pass anytime.

You're doing awesome, Dad. The kids in your program are lucky to have you!

Amanda

subject: Thanksgiving in Houston
snippet: Amanda, I wanted to start planning early so we have enough time to sort every...
body:
Amanda, I wanted to start planning early so we have enough time to sort everything out. Your mother wants the whole family in Houston for Thanksgiving this year - the 26th of November - and she's already talking about a big weekend of cooking. Ada and Obinna are bringing little Chisom, so it'll be a full house.

Can you check your work calendar and let me know which travel dates work? Flights from Greensboro fill up fast around the holidays.

Emeka

subject: Re: Thanksgiving in Houston
snippet: Hi Dad, Thanksgiving in Houston sounds perfect - I'll block off that whole w...
body:
Hi Dad, Thanksgiving in Houston sounds perfect - I'll block off that whole week so no clients can pull me in. I'll probably fly in the Tuesday before, the 24th, and stay through the weekend.

My Ashdale Q4 audit wraps in late October and I've got the Charlotte cybersecurity forum on the 17th and 18th, but nothing after that, so the timing works. I can't wait to see Chisom, she must be so big now.

Should I bring anything, or is Mom running the kitchen like always?

Amanda

subject: Re: Thanksgiving in Houston
snippet: Tuesday the 24th is perfect - that gives us a couple of days to settle in be...
body:
Tuesday the 24th is perfect - that gives us a couple of days to settle in before the big day. Book your flight soon though, the Greensboro-to-Houston fares climb fast this time of year.

Mom is definitely running the kitchen, but she said you're on jollof duty since yours has been better than hers lately (don't tell her I said the second part). Ada is bringing dessert.

And bring that little studio speaker of yours - the whole family wants to hear Lagos Sunset properly, not off a phone. See you soon!

Emeka

subject: Re: Thanksgiving in Houston
snippet: Ha, jollof duty accepted - challenge is on. I'll bring the spice blend I use...
body:
Ha, jollof duty accepted - challenge is on. I'll bring the spice blend I use so nobody complains it's too smoky this time.

Booking my flight into Houston tonight for the 24th. And yes, I'll pack the studio monitor so everyone can actually hear Lagos Sunset the way Yemi and I mixed it. Might sneak in a rough of the next single too if it's ready.

Should I grab anything for Chisom? I saw some little drums that light up and thought of her instantly.

Amanda

subject: Re: Thanksgiving in Houston
snippet: Chisom will lose her mind over light-up drums - perfect, that's her gift sor...
body:
Chisom will lose her mind over light-up drums - perfect, that's her gift sorted. Just be ready, once she has drums she does not stop.

Your mother is thrilled you're coming in early. She's already writing out the cooking schedule like it's a military operation. I'll leave the guest room made up for you.

I'm really looking forward to this. It's been too long since the whole family was under one roof. See you in Houston on the 24th!

Emeka

subject: Service Renewal - Need Your Help
snippet: Hi Amanda, I hope you're doing well. I got a notice from the home security compa...
body:
Hi Amanda, I hope you're doing well. I got a notice from the home security company saying my annual plan is up for renewal next week. The price went up again this year - they're asking for $289, which seems like a lot more than what I paid last time.

I remember you mentioned there might be a better deal if I call and ask, or maybe switch to a different plan. Do you have a few minutes this week to help me sort this out? I don't want to just let it auto-renew if there's a cheaper option.

Let me know what works for you. Thanks, Dad

subject: Re: Service Renewal - Need Your Help
snippet: Hi Dad, yes, definitely don't let it auto-renew at that price. I looked it up an...
body:
Hi Dad, yes, definitely don't let it auto-renew at that price. They usually offer a loyalty discount if you just call and ask - sometimes up to 20% off. Just tell them you're considering canceling and they'll transfer you to the retention team.

While you've got them on the phone, ask them to turn on two-factor login for the app too - I keep telling you and Mom, that's the part that actually keeps someone from remotely watching your cameras. If they won't budge on price, I can recommend a couple of alternatives I trust from my own work.

Let's talk it through on our Sunday call. Talk soon, Amanda

subject: Stonewick pentest + Sunday
snippet: Amanda, I hope you're doing well. Ada mentioned you've got a big pentest comi...
body:
Amanda, I hope you're doing well. Ada mentioned you've got a big pentest coming up for one of your Charlotte clients. I don't fully understand the technical side, but she said it sounded like a lot of work. Will you have any time to breathe this month?

Your mother wants to know if you're coming for the Sunday call and whether we should just talk then or if you can visit. She found some old photos from when you first started making beats - I can dig them out if you come by.

- Emeka

subject: Re: Stonewick pentest + Sunday
snippet: Hi Dad, yes, the Stonewick pentest is the big one this month - it's their an...
body:
Hi Dad, yes, the Stonewick pentest is the big one this month - it's their annual external test, so I'll be heads-down for a couple of weeks. Nothing scary, just a lot of late nights with my tools. I'll still make the Sunday call, I always do.

I'll try to drive up to see you and Mom the following Saturday afternoon once the report's out. Tell her I'll eat whatever she makes, no fuss. And yes, please dig out those old studio photos - I'd love to see them!

- Amanda

subject: Re: Stonewick pentest + Sunday
snippet: That makes sense. Ada made it sound very complicated! I'm glad it's going we...
body:
That makes sense. Ada made it sound very complicated! I'm glad it's going well - you've always been good with these things, even when you were little and always fixing the computer for me.

Saturday is perfect. Your mother is already planning jollof rice and says she wants to hear the new track properly this time. Drive carefully and call when you leave Greensboro.

- Emeka

subject: Lagos Sunset hook
snippet: Yemi, the Lagos Sunset hook is done. Sending the reference bounce now - tel...
body: Yemi, the Lagos Sunset hook is done. Sending the reference bounce now - tell me if the tempo feels right at 102 BPM. If you like it I'll get Sage on the mix next week.

body: Hey Amanda, it's Marcus from Brightmoor - we passed the SOC2 Type II with your evidence pack. Auditor had zero exceptions. Thank you, sending final payment today.
